note: Universal default parameters for signal timing calculation. Country modules may override specific values.

data.notes: 3s for speeds <= 60 km/h, 4s for 70-80 km/h, 5s for > 80 km/h. Kenya RDM 6.3 Table 14.3 provides speed-specific values.
Calculate from R = (W + L_veh) / v_approach. Minimum 1 second.
7s minimum to allow vehicles between stop line and detector to clear. 10-15s where no detection.
Walk interval (steady Walk signal). Minimum 4-7 seconds for step-off. Total pedestrian green = Walk + Clearance.
Flashing Don't Walk period. Calculated as W / v_walk. Use 1.2 m/s standard, 0.8 m/s for elderly/disabled.

data.depends_on: lane width, gradient, turning, HGV proportion
data.notes: 1800 pcu/hr/lane for standard 3.0-3.5 m lane, flat grade, no turning, no HGV adjustment.
Start-up lost time at the beginning of green. Typically 2-3 seconds.
120s practical maximum. 150s absolute maximum. > 120s causes excessive pedestrian delay.
Below 25-30s, the proportion of lost time is excessive and intersection efficiency drops.
1.2 m/s for standard design. Use 0.8-1.0 m/s near hospitals, aged care, schools for young children.
Used in all-red calculation. 6 m represents a standard car/light truck.
